```python
class Solution:
    def maximumXOR(self, nums: List[int]) -> int:
        ans = 0
        for i in range(30, -1, -1):
            ans <<= 1
            curr = ans | 1
            prefixes = {num >> i for num in nums}
            ans |= any((curr ^ p) in prefixes for p in prefixes)
        return ans
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(N)
space complexity: O(N)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The solution iterates over the bits of the numbers from most significant to least, maintaining a running XOR result. It checks if setting the current bit to 1 would result in a valid XOR operation by verifying if the corresponding prefix exists in the set of prefixes. If so, it sets the bit to 1, effectively maximizing the XOR result.
